Referee Richard J. Wall'S Motion Confirming Referee'S Report
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Continued To Jun-14-13 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 502

Referee Richard J. Wall'S Motion Confirming Referee'S Report
Off Calendar Jun-14-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Referee Richard J. Wall; no appearance by Plaintiffs or counsel. Defendant Eugene Chune present in pro per with real estate agent Patrick Lam. Prospective buyers: Patrick Barber present with agent Joan Smith, and Nina Geneson present in pro per.

The Court hears Referee Richard J. Wall's Motion Confirming Referee's Report. Sale of the subject property is conducted with final sale in the amount of $1,184,450.00 to the over bidder Nina Geneson. Mr. Eugene Chune agreed to vacate the subject property with in 60 days (5 pm. on 08-15-13) in exchange for $20,000.00 pay out to be placed in an Escrow Account. Binding agreement is signed by the parties in open court, Mr. Wall to retain the original signed document and to prepare an order of the final sale and submit it to Dept. 502 for signature.

Dept. 502, Judge R James Robertson, Clerk S. Ravinantapricha, not reported.

Referee Richard J. Wall'S Motion Confirming Referee'S Report

Atty. Amy P.H. Luk of Law Offices of Amy P.H. Luk (415-379-9139) appear for defendant Eugene chune defendant but no substitution of attorney on record.
Referee Richard J. Wall (415-434-3323)

The court vacate tentative ruling, Referee Richard J. Wall'S Motion Confirming Referee's Report is continued to June 14, 2013 at 1:30pm. Referee Richard J. Wall give notices. Judge R James Robertson, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on defendant's motion to set aside the interlocutary judgment issued September 22, 2009, set aside default/default judgment, and for final judgment.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Defendant Eugene Chune was present in pro per, placed under oath and examined.
Christina Chune and Michael Chung, witnesses for defendant, were placed under oath but did not testify.

Order was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this for hearing on plaintiffs' request for entry of final judgment, and defendant's motion to set aside the default issued May 19, 2008, and interlocutory judgment issued September 22, 2009.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Norman Lew appeared for plaintiffs Bernice Chune, Grace Chune and Leland Chune, who were not present at today's hearing.
John Chow, Esq. and Andrew Nguyen, Esq. appeared for defendant Eugene Chune, who was not present at today's hearing.

Default Prove-Up/Partition Action - Real Property
Off Calendar Sep-22-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Norman Lew, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Bernice Chune, who was placed under under oath and examined.
Mr. Lew appeared for plaintiffs Grace Chune and Leland Chune, who were not present at today's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Eugene Chune.
Default was entered against defendant on May 19, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, September 22, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Bernice Chune, Grace Chune and Leland Chune, and against the Defaulted Defendant Eugne Chune as follows:

Plaintiffs Bernice Chune, Grace Chune and Leland Chune, along with defendant Eugene Chune, are each owner of an undivided twenty-five percent (25%) interest in the real property located at 143 Varennes Street, San Francisco, CA. Said property shall be sold, and the proceeds shall be divided between the three plaintiffs and defendant in equal shares, after payment of the expenses connected with the sale of the property, attorney fees and payment to the referee. Kimba Chu is appointed referee, and has the authority to sell the property at public auction to the highest bidder for cash, except as may be otherwise provided by Sections 873.600-873.690 of the Code of Civil Procedure.

Matter is continued to January 28, 2010 @ 9:00 a.m. in Department 218 for hearing re: final judgment.

Interlocutory Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
